seoTitle: Paradise Point vs Park Avenue — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Paradise Point and Park Avenue. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Paradise Point vs Park Avenue: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Paradise Point and Park Avenue.
heading: Paradise Point vs Park Avenue — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Paradise Point, Queensland and Park Avenue, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Paradise Point has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Park Avenue (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Paradise Point or Park Avenue safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Paradise Point has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Park Avenue scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Paradise Point scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Park Avenue scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Paradise Point or Park Avenue?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Paradise Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
